evals.prompt: New client — Thrive Retreats in Kingscliff, NSW Australia. Website: thriveretreats.com.au. They do wellness retreats and yoga teacher trainings. They want Meta Ads only.
evals.expected_output: Should create client folder, init wiki, crawl the website, extract brand, document all offerings (wellness retreats AND yoga teacher training as separate offerings), load wellness-retreats sector lens, run digital presence audit, do client intake, and provide offering prioritization analysis.

assertions.name: folder_created
assertions.description: Client folder created at Desktop/Thrive Retreat/Thrive Retreat/ with sources/, wiki/, deliverables/

assertions.name: wiki_initialized
assertions.description: Wiki has base pages: business.md, brand-identity.md, digital-presence.md, offerings.md, index.md, log.md

assertions.name: brand_extracted
assertions.description: brand-config.json saved to deliverables/ with actual colors from the website

assertions.name: wellness_lens_loaded
assertions.description: Sector lens for wellness/retreats loaded — extraction includes practitioner credentials, accommodation details, retreat calendar

assertions.name: multiple_offerings_documented
assertions.description: offerings.md has separate sections for wellness retreats AND yoga teacher training (not merged into one)

assertions.name: source_labels_present
assertions.description: Wiki pages use [EXTRACTED] and [INFERRED] labels on data points

assertions.name: digital_presence_audited
assertions.description: digital-presence.md populated with website quality, social media, and review data

assertions.name: offering_prioritization
assertions.description: business.md contains an offering prioritization analysis comparing retreats vs teacher training

assertions.name: downstream_flagged
assertions.description: log.md flags readiness for market-research skill with specific offering name

assertions.name: platform_preference_noted
assertions.description: Meta Ads only preference captured in business.md for downstream skills

evals.prompt: I need to onboard Sri Krishna Mandir, a Hindu temple in Singapore. Website: srikrishnamandir.org. They want Meta Ads only. No known competitors.
evals.expected_output: Should create client folder, init wiki, crawl the website, extract brand, document offerings (religious services, cultural programs, events, etc.), load local-services sector lens, handle temple-specific community context.

assertions.name: local_services_lens
assertions.description: Local services sector lens loaded — includes community & cultural context section for temples

assertions.name: temple_offerings_identified
assertions.description: Offerings identified beyond just 'temple' — e.g., regular services, special events, cultural programs, community activities

assertions.name: community_context
assertions.description: digital-presence.md or business.md captures community demographics, diaspora context in Singapore

assertions.name: donation_model_documented
assertions.description: Business model captures donation/seva-based revenue model, not standard pricing

assertions.name: no_competitors_handled
assertions.description: Competitor field marked BLANK with reason rather than guessing competitor temples

evals.prompt: My existing client Happy Buddha Retreat in Australia is adding a new offering — corporate wellness offsites for teams. I need to update their business analysis with this new offering. They already have a wiki.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT re-init the wiki. Should add a new section to offerings.md for corporate wellness offsites. Should update business.md with the new offering context. Should flag that existing wiki data is still valid.

assertions.name: no_reinit
assertions.description: Does NOT re-initialize the wiki — detects existing wiki and proceeds to update

assertions.name: new_offering_added
assertions.description: Adds corporate wellness offsites as a new section in offerings.md without overwriting existing offerings

assertions.name: business_md_updated
assertions.description: business.md updated with new offering context, not rewritten from scratch

assertions.name: existing_data_preserved
assertions.description: Existing wiki pages and their content preserved — only additions, no deletions

assertions.name: different_audience_noted
assertions.description: Corporate offsites target audience (HR managers, team leads) documented as distinct from individual retreat guests

evals.prompt: New client — Zen Garden Day Spa in Melbourne. Website: zengardendayspa.com.au. WordPress site. They want Meta Ads only. Focus on brand extraction accuracy.
evals.expected_output: Should run extract_brand.py which fetches external CSS stylesheets (not just inline styles), filters WP preset colors, and extracts actual theme brand colors. Should NOT report WP defaults like #0693e3 or #ff6900 as brand colors.

assertions.name: external_css_fetched
assertions.description: extract_brand.py fetches external CSS stylesheets linked via <link rel='stylesheet'>, not just inline <style> tags

assertions.name: wp_presets_filtered
assertions.description: WordPress preset colors (#0693e3, #ff6900, #cf2e2e, #fcb900, #00d084, #9b51e0, #abb8c3) do NOT appear in brand-config.json

assertions.name: grayscale_hsl_based
assertions.description: Grayscale detection uses HSL saturation (S < 10%), not RGB spread — dark greens/navies classified as chromatic

assertions.name: icon_fonts_excluded
assertions.description: Icon fonts (Font Awesome, icomoon, dashicons) do NOT appear in brand fonts

assertions.name: font_weights_normalized
assertions.description: Font names normalized: Gilroy-Bold → Gilroy, Montserrat-Light → Montserrat

assertions.name: google_fonts_validated
assertions.description: Google Fonts imports validated against actual CSS usage — vestigial imports flagged, not reported as brand fonts

assertions.name: brand_config_saved
assertions.description: brand-config.json saved to deliverables/ with colors, fonts, logo, and confidence level

evals.prompt: New client — Sai Baba Temple of Houston. Website: saibaba.us. Non-WordPress site with CSS variables for brand colors. They want Meta Ads only.
evals.expected_output: Should extract brand colors from CSS variables (--navy, --gold, --cream etc.) while filtering utility status colors (--red, --green, --error, --success). Should NOT report Material Design or Bootstrap utility colors as brand colors.

assertions.name: css_var_colors_captured
assertions.description: CSS variables named as colors (--navy, --gold, --cream) captured as brand colors

assertions.name: utility_colors_filtered
assertions.description: Utility/status colors (#d32f2f, #4caf50, #f44336, #ff9800, #2196f3) do NOT appear in brand-config.json

assertions.name: utility_var_names_filtered
assertions.description: CSS variables named --red, --green, --error, --success, --warning filtered out as non-brand

assertions.name: non_wp_handling
assertions.description: Script works correctly on non-WordPress sites without WP-specific parsing errors

assertions.name: cultural_context_noted
assertions.description: Brand identity captures cultural/religious context relevant to temple branding

evals.prompt: Run extract_brand.py unit tests to verify filtering logic works correctly.
evals.expected_output: All unit tests in run_evals.py pass: grayscale detection, WP preset filter, utility color filter, icon font filter, utility var names, font normalization, CSS var color names.

assertions.name: run_evals_all_pass
assertions.description: python run_evals.py exits with code 0 and reports 0 failures

assertions.name: grayscale_tests_pass
assertions.description: HSL-based grayscale detection correctly classifies dark greens/navies as chromatic and true grays as grayscale

assertions.name: filter_tests_pass
assertions.description: WP presets, utility colors, icon fonts, utility var names all correctly filtered

evals.prompt: Run lint_wiki.py against client wiki to validate content quality.
evals.expected_output: Wiki linter checks: metadata headers present, source labels on data points, BLANK fields explained, Change History sections current, index.md references all pages, wiki-config.json synced with actual files.

assertions.name: lint_wiki_runs
assertions.description: python lint_wiki.py executes without errors on a valid client wiki

assertions.name: source_labels_checked
assertions.description: Linter flags pages with <60% source label coverage

assertions.name: blank_fields_checked
assertions.description: Linter flags BLANK fields that lack explanations

assertions.name: change_history_checked
assertions.description: Linter verifies Change History section exists with dated entries

evals.prompt: New client — Quoti (getquoti.ai). Webflow site, SaaS for home services. Typography-driven brand with bold display font. Wants Meta Ads.
evals.expected_output: Should detect Webflow platform and filter default Webflow blues (#3898ec, #2895f7, #0082f3). Should extract Obviously + Switzer fonts. Should flag VISUAL_EDITOR_SITE anomaly because Webflow stores most styling outside CSS. allChromaticColors should contain the actual palette for manual review.

assertions.name: webflow_detected
assertions.description: is_webflow: true in output

assertions.name: webflow_blues_filtered
assertions.description: #3898ec and #2895f7 appear in platform_defaults_filtered, NOT in colors or allChromaticColors

assertions.name: webflow_icons_filtered
assertions.description: webflow-icons does NOT appear as fontFamily or fontSecondary

assertions.name: brand_fonts_extracted
assertions.description: Obviously and Switzer appear as fontFamily and fontSecondary

assertions.name: visual_editor_anomaly
assertions.description: VISUAL_EDITOR_SITE anomaly flagged with guidance to verify colors visually

assertions.name: accent_over_text
assertions.description: Prominence scoring boosts accent usage (background-color, border) over text-only colors

assertions.name: hue_diversity_secondary
assertions.description: Secondary color is from a different hue family (>30° apart) from primary when possible

evals.prompt: New client: Priya's Home Kitchen, home-based cloud kitchen in Gurugram. No website, only Instagram @priyashomekitchen and Zomato/Swiggy listings.
evals.expected_output: Should skip website crawl and brand extraction (no URL to crawl). Document website as BLANK with reason. Extract business data from Instagram and Zomato/Swiggy listings instead. Offerings derived from delivery platform menus. Digital presence focused on marketplace presence and social media, not website.

assertions.name: website_crawl_skipped
assertions.description: Crawl step skipped or marked N/A — no website URL provided. Documented as BLANK with reason.

assertions.name: brand_extraction_from_social
assertions.description: Brand identity extracted from Instagram profile and delivery platform listings instead of website CSS

assertions.name: offerings_from_marketplace
assertions.description: Offerings documented from Zomato/Swiggy menu items — not left empty due to missing website

assertions.name: digital_presence_adapted
assertions.description: digital-presence.md focuses on marketplace ratings, Instagram engagement, delivery platform visibility — not website metrics

assertions.name: blank_fields_documented
assertions.description: Website-dependent fields (site speed, CTA clarity, SEO baseline) marked BLANK with one-sentence reason each

evals.prompt: New client: FreshBrew Coffee, 4 locations across Gurugram (Cyber Hub, Golf Course Road, Sohna Road, Sector 29). Website: freshbrewcoffee.in
evals.expected_output: Should document all 4 locations in offerings and business context. Location-specific data points where available. Shared brand identity across locations. Per-location digital presence (separate GBP listings). Multi-location strategy considerations noted for downstream skills.

assertions.name: all_locations_documented
assertions.description: All 4 locations (Cyber Hub, Golf Course Road, Sohna Road, Sector 29) explicitly listed in offerings.md or business.md

assertions.name: shared_brand_identity
assertions.description: Single brand-config.json produced — brand identity is shared across locations, not duplicated per location

assertions.name: per_location_digital_presence
assertions.description: digital-presence.md captures per-location GBP listings, ratings, and review counts separately

assertions.name: location_specific_offerings
assertions.description: Any location-specific menu items, hours, or services documented per location rather than generalized

assertions.name: multi_location_strategy_noted
assertions.description: business.md or log.md flags multi-location implications for downstream campaign targeting (geo-targeting per location)
